content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob have an array A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ of integers between −10100-10^{100}−10100 and 1010010^{100}10100 inclusive.

Initially, all of the array elements are unknown to you.

You have to determine whether the information that Alice and Bob give you is consistent, or that you are certain that at least one of them is lying.

Alice first tells you MMM pieces of information.

Each piece of information that Alice gives you is of the form (pos,val)(pos,val)(pos,val), which means she tells you that Apos=valA_{pos}=valApos​=val.

Bob then tells you KKK pieces of information.

Each piece of information that Bob gives you is of the form (u,v,d)(u,v,d)(u,v,d), which means he tells you that ∣Au−Av∣≤d|A_u-A_v| \le d∣Au​−Av​∣≤d.

Determine whether such an array which satisfies all M+KM+KM+K pieces of information exists.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK — the number of elements of array AAA, the number of information given by Alice, and the number of information given by Bob.
The next MMM lines describe the information from Alice. Each of these MMM lines contains two space-separated integers pospospos and valvalval respectively - denoting that Apos=valA_{pos}=valApos​=val.
The next KKK lines describe the information from Bob. Each of these KKK lines contains three space-separated integers uuu, vvv, and ddd respectively - denoting that ∣Au−Av∣≤d|A_u-A_v| \le d∣Au​−Av​∣≤d.




Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if such an array which is consistent with all M+KM+KM+K information from Alice and Bob exists, and `NO` otherwise.


Each letter of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `no`, `NO`, `No`, `nO` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: 1≤pos≤N1 \le pos \le N1≤pos≤N for all information from Alice
−109≤val≤109-10^9 \le val \le 10^9−109≤val≤109 for all information from Alice
1≤u,v≤N1 \le u,v \le N1≤u,v≤N for all information from Bob
0≤d≤1090 \le d \le 10^90≤d≤109 for all information from Bob
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 200020002000.
The sum of KKK over all test cases won't exceed 300030003000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, the only valid array is A=[−1,1,3]A=[-1,1,3]A=[−1,1,3].
In the third test case, the only valid array is A=[2,−1,1,0]A=[2,-1,1,0]A=[2,−1,1,0].
In the fourth test case, A1A_1A1​ can take any integer value from −10100-10^{100}−10100 to 1010010^{100}10100.
We can prove that there are no such valid arrays in the second test case.
